episodes.title: How I (Accidentally) Escaped a Cult | my story with Monastic Academy
episodes.description:
A few years ago I needed a break from my company. I went to the Monastic Academy for the Preservation of Life on Earth to meditate for a few months. On day five of a silent retreat I accidentally escaped a cult.

I mean "cult" literally. It names a way of thinking so common that most people no longer notice it, let alone notice that it is causing the destruction of life on earth. It has charismatic figures, conversion rituals, and severe consequences for leaving. Most of its members would deny they are in a cult at all, because from inside the cult it's just the way things are.

I spent years moving through the communities that publicly claim to be addressing the destruction of life on Earth and the disconnection between people. I spent time in Silicon Valley, in effective altruism, and in adjacent rationalist and existential-risk circles, starting companies and consulting and looking for the place where these problems were being met at the root. These were the people working hardest on the problems. They were the people most captured by the cult of data. The lives-per-dollar calculation runs on the same operating system as the Instagram feed. The mind of data cannot be cut through by clinging to more data.

The cult of data is the mind that turns the person in front of you into a profile. It is inseparable from climate collapse and from the AI systems now finding exploitable vulnerabilities across the browsers and operating systems underlying much of the Internet. The window in which a different mind can be cultivated at scale is closing.

When I refer to this mind as a cult, I will get pushback. That pushback refuses to take a side. It assumes the people destroying the world are as right as the people opposing them. That hedging is the cult's way of denying responsibility. Monasteries organized around the training of mind are not destroying life on Earth. Almost everything else in the modern attention economy is.

Individual practice cannot oppose the cult. Only a residential monastic community can. MAPLE is that community.

We are opening a three-month residency for 12 people. Apply below. It's likely to fill fast.

https://cohort.monasticacademy.org/code/escape-the-cult
